title: ullucus tuberosus subsp. aborigineus
intro: Ullucus tuberosus subsp. aborigineus, also known as "papa amarga" or "bitter potato," is a subspecies of the ullucus tuberosus plant native to South America, specifically in the Andean regions of Peru and Bolivia. The roots of Ullucus tuberosus subsp. aborigineus have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ullucus tuberosus subsp. aborigineus include its ability to help lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable herb in treating various health conditions. Ullucus tuberosus subsp. aborigineus is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, the roots are dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
